title: France Health Advice
description: Essential health information for travelers to France
content:
## Vaccinations
- Routine vaccines: Make sure you are up-to-date on all routine vaccines before every trip
- Hepatitis A: Recommended for most travelers
- Hepatitis B: Recommended for travelers who might have sex with a new partner, get a tattoo or piercing, or have any medical procedures
- Rabies: Recommended for travelers involved in outdoor and other activities in remote areas that put them at risk for bat bites

## Health Risks
- Air quality: Generally good in most areas, but can be poor in urban centers during summer
- Water quality: Tap water is safe to drink throughout France
- Food safety: High standards in restaurants and food establishments

## Medical Facilities
- Excellent healthcare system with modern facilities
- Pharmacies (pharmacie) are widely available and marked with a green cross
- Emergency number: 112 (EU-wide) or 15 (medical emergency)

## Prescription Medications
- Bring sufficient supply of any prescription medications
- Carry medications in original packaging with prescription copy
- Some medications available over-the-counter in Europe may require prescription in France

title: Japan Health Advice
description: Essential health information for travelers to Japan
content:
## Vaccinations
- Routine vaccines: Make sure you are up-to-date on all routine vaccines before every trip
- Hepatitis A: Recommended for most travelers
- Hepatitis B: Recommended for travelers who might have sex with a new partner, get a tattoo or piercing, or have any medical procedures
- Japanese Encephalitis: Recommended for travelers spending a month or more in rural areas or those spending significant time outdoors in rural areas
- Rabies: Recommended for travelers involved in outdoor and other activities in remote areas that put them at risk for bat bites

## Health Risks
- Air quality: Generally good in most areas
- Water quality: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Japan
- Food safety: Very high standards, but be cautious with raw foods

## Medical Facilities
- Excellent healthcare system with modern facilities
- Pharmacies: Look for signs in Japanese (薬局 or kusuriya)
- Emergency number: 119 (fire/ambulance) or 110 (police)

## Prescription Medications
- Some common medications available in other countries may be restricted or unavailable in Japan
- Check with Japanese embassy about bringing specific medications
- Consider bringing a detailed doctor's note for any prescription medications
